sprint: Object Strip Becomes the ONE Reorder Control — Card Designer Buttons Removed + Lock Badge Added
task: Direct product feedback after trying the shipped Object Strip drag-to-reorder: 'i like the reordering function in object strip. 2 recommendations. 1st remove any reordering function from the right panel. 2nd for an object which cannot be reordered, put a lock or some kind of information show.' Recommendation 1: removed Card Designer's Sticker/Frame/Decoration Order button rows (Back/Backward/Forward/Front, Backward/Forward) outright from js/cardDesigner.js -- a second, now-redundant way to reorder once the Object Strip's drag already did it better -- along with the shared _reorderSelected(edge, commitFn) helper they called (now genuinely dead code, removed rather than left orphaned) and Frame's own _frameAdjustZ wrapper; SlideRenderer.getReorderableIds()/SceneEngine.setLayerOrder() are completely unchanged, since the Object Strip's drag handler already reads/writes them directly. Removed the matching now-unused .sticker-layer-*/.frame-layer-*/.decoration-layer-* CSS from css/style.css, carefully stripping only the -layer- selectors out of combined rules so the still-used Sticker Flip button styling stayed untouched. Recommendation 2: a real object (has its own render-tree id) that isn't in the current reorderable set -- a locked/non-moveable World-owned Layer Pack object -- now gets a small lock badge on its Object Strip thumbnail (js/objectStrip.js's _card(), new .object-card-lock-badge CSS), positioned opposite the existing edit-capability badge, plus a Can't be reordered tooltip; gated on a real id so the two synthetic Background/Artwork-Place cards never show it. Verified via a new Playwright test (a locked World Shape shows the lock badge, a reorderable Sticker and the synthetic Background card show none) plus two existing tests updated to replace their now-intentionally-broken button-click assertions with equivalent confirm-buttons-are-gone / real-drag-based reorder assertions. Full regression across the entire existing Creator/Builder/Card-Platform Playwright suite (22 scripts) and both goldenBuild.js suites passes unchanged, zero console errors. See CLAUDE.md's matching entry for the full account.
